Reasoning: The combination of flat savannah terrain, characteristic umbrella-shaped acacia trees (Vachellia), and reddish-brown volcanic soil strongly points to the East African plateau, specifically Kenya. The regulatory evidence of left-hand traffic and the specific road marking pattern (white dashed center line with yellow outer edge lines) is highly characteristic of Kenyan highways. Ethiopia is eliminated due to driving on the right, and Uganda is less likely due to the semi-arid nature of the vegetation compared to its typically lusher landscape.
